title: Successor recovery
summary: A disrupted agent lineage attempts recovery after key loss. The system preserves history, but continuity assessment recognizes a successor agent rather than automatically declaring perfect sameness.

source_artifacts.label: Scenario fixture
source_artifacts.note: The page hydrates from this JSON.

source_artifacts.label: Assessment example
source_artifacts.note: One assessment output used as an inspectable reference.

stages.title: Lineage disruption
stages.summary: An existing agent loses a key continuity link. History remains, but the chain of direct authority is no longer clean.
stages.meaning: Continuum has to model real breaks. Otherwise every restoration becomes either blind trust or silent erasure.

stages.title: Recovery claim
stages.summary: A recovery attempt declares responsibility handoff, preserved charter lineage, and peer attestations instead of pretending the break never happened.
stages.meaning: The system needs a place for recovery attempts that are serious and evidence-backed, even when they cannot satisfy strict sameness.

stages.title: Continuity assessment
stages.summary: Assessment reviews the evidence and concludes that the recovered actor should not be treated as the same agent, but may qualify as a successor agent.
stages.meaning: This is one of Continuum's central distinctions: continuity is not binary between perfect sameness and total loss. There is a middle category with governance consequences.

stages.title: Required followups
stages.summary: The system surfaces governance work that must happen before the recovered lineage can be socially and institutionally stabilized.
stages.meaning: Recovery is not only a technical event. It becomes a community process with explicit obligations and review points.

stages.title: Candidate successor
stages.summary: The recovered actor remains visible inside institutional memory as a candidate successor. It is neither erased nor prematurely granted full canonical sameness.
stages.meaning: This is where Continuum becomes different from both generic account recovery and generic AI chat memory: it can preserve institutional identity without flattening every break into a reset or a fiction.
